title: Observability, Service Levels, Support, and Incident Learning · Claim Repair Board
summary: A preserved decision record for observability, service levels, support, and incident learning contains one plausible evidence, scope, design, implementation, evaluation, or ownership defect. Find the first unsupported claim before changing the product. Planned artifact: Repair a changed failure in user-centered operations and incident system and publish the reproduction, evidence, repair, and regression record. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal…
objectives: Choose user-centered availability, correctness, latency, freshness, durability, or support indicators with valid measurement boundaries.
Set objective, window, population, exclusions, error budget, and response policy from user need and operating capacity.
Correlate revision, request, actor class, workflow, dependency, event, log, metric, and trace without high-cardinality or privacy leakage.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.config:
# personal-project-2 cumulative independent product evidence portfolio
# Browser labs perform deterministic evidence-contract review only.
# They never contact participants or services, execute commands, deploy, or claim live conformance.
workspace: portfolio
project-id: personal-project-2
stakeholder: [replace with affected people and accountable decision owner]
decision: [replace with the decision this evidence changes]
problem-evidence: [replace with observed need, counterevidence, and uncertainty]
accessibility: [replace with keyboard, structure, reflow, status, alternatives, and human review]
security: [replace with threat, authorization, abuse, response, and residual risk]
privacy: [replace with purpose, minimization, retention, access, correction, and deletion]
test-strategy: [replace with behavior, changed case, fault, accessibility, security, and recovery]
release-evidence: [replace with revision, artifact, inventory, provenance, validation, and rollback]
recovery: [replace with backup, clean restore, reconciliation, communication, and ownership]
portfolio-proof: [replace with contribution, limitations, counterexample, and unseen defense]
transfer-boundary: authorized controlled research, toolchain, deployment, and review environments
evidence:

steps.title: Inspect the failure record · Debug Service Level Indicator
steps.instruction:
debug case — Many logs report healthy servers while users cannot complete the task and support cannot correlate the failure with actor, workflow, dependency, or revision. The debug variation 5 changes the stakeholder, access need, evidence source, constraint, failure, and operating environment. Competency probe: Choose user-centered availability, correctness, latency, freshness, durability, or support indicators with valid measurement boundaries.

Inspect the Service Level Indicator trace for this debug case. Which record connects a precondition, observed state change, and boundary result?

Evidence target: Inspect the failure record · Debug Service Level Indicator.
steps.why: The decision supplies observable evidence for choose user-centered availability, correctness, latency, freshness, durability, or support indicators with valid measurement boundaries. For Inspect the failure record · Debug Service Level Indicator, keep Service Level Indicator connected to mechanically reviewable stakeholder, affected-user, artifact, changed-case, failure, repair, release, recovery, and transfer decision evidence.

content.text: For Service Level Indicator, distinguish a claim from evidence by locating the relevant precondition, trace or invariant, and changed input result.

hints: For Inspect the failure record · Debug Service Level Indicator, name the types, ownership, and stop path, Service Level Indicator precondition, or invariant that must hold.

At Inspect the failure record · Debug Service Level Indicator, trace this concrete input and predict values, errors, blocking, and output before reviewing it: Many logs report healthy servers while users cannot complete the task and support cannot correlate the failure with actor, workflow, dependency, or revision. The debug variation 5 changes the stakeholder, access need, evidence source, constraint, failure, and operating environment. Competency probe: Choose user-centered availability, correctness, latency, freshness, durability, or support indicators with valid measurement boundaries.

For Inspect the failure record · Debug Service Level Indicator, challenge the documented misconception with runtime, failure, race, or toolchain evidence: Server uptime directly measures user success.

stimulus.title: Service Level Indicator case record
stimulus.caption: Three excerpts from a learner evidence packet; only one connects a claim to a reproducible check.

lines.label: assumption
lines.text: Many logs report healthy servers while users cannot complete the task and support cannot correlate the failure with actor, workflow, dependency, or revision. The debug variation 5 changes the stakeholder, access need, evidence source, constraint, failure, and operating environment. Competency probe: Choose user-centered availability, correctness, latency, freshness, durability, or support indicators with valid measurement boundaries.

lines.label: risk
lines.text: Server uptime directly measures user success.

lines.label: evidence
lines.text: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Choose user-centered availability, correctness, latency, freshness, durability, or support indicators with valid measurement boundaries. Artifact under review: user-centered operations and incident system.

options.text: The record names the assumption, applies Service Level Indicator, and reports a changed-case check tied to the stakeholder decision.

The Service Level Indicator record says user-centered operations and incident system looks right but supplies no governing condition, observation, changed case, or counterexample. That claim fails the inspect the failure record evidence boundary for Service Level Indicator.

For inspect the failure record, apply Service Level Indicator without its stated preconditions, omit the user-centered operations and incident system trace and counterexample, and treat one plausible output as complete proof. This does not satisfy Service Level Indicator for user-centered operations and incident system.

steps.title: Implement the cause-level repair · Debug Incident Learning
steps.instruction:
Append a pp2-incident-learning-z8nsxz: manifest entry with concrete verify plus failure, owner, or repair evidence.

Evidence target: Implement the cause-level repair · Debug Incident Learning.
steps.why: Building a runnable or mechanically reviewable artifact turns Incident Learning into observable performance evidence. For Implement the cause-level repair · Debug Incident Learning, keep Incident Learning connected to mechanically reviewable stakeholder, affected-user, artifact, changed-case, failure, repair, release, recovery, and transfer decision evidence.

content.code:
pp2-incident-learning-z8nsxz:
  verify: [replace with a deterministic check]
  failure: [replace with the rejected condition]
  repair: [replace with a local reproduction and correction path]
content.caption: Shape of the required increment; replace generic language with the current stakeholder case.

content.title: Evidence is more than syntax
content.text: The Incident Learning structural check is only one gate. user-centered operations and incident system must also survive the stated changed case and causal explanation.

hints: For Implement the cause-level repair · Debug Incident Learning, name the types, ownership, and stop path, Incident Learning precondition, or invariant that must hold.

At Implement the cause-level repair · Debug Incident Learning, trace this concrete input and predict values, errors, blocking, and output before reviewing it: Many logs report healthy servers while users cannot complete the task and support cannot correlate the failure with actor, workflow, dependency, or revision. The debug variation 6 changes the stakeholder, access need, evidence source, constraint, failure, and operating environment. Competency probe: Preserve timeline and evidence, restore safely, communicate impact, analyze contributing conditions without blame, and track preventive work.

For Implement the cause-level repair · Debug Incident Learning, challenge the documented misconception with runtime, failure, race, or toolchain evidence: The person who made the triggering change is the incident root cause.

steps.title: Publish the incident record · Debug Incident Learning
steps.instruction:
Write the debug evidence note for Incident Learning: explain the governing condition, one failure, and how this concrete case was verified — Many logs report healthy servers while users cannot complete the task and support cannot correlate the failure with actor, workflow, dependency, or revision. The debug variation 3 changes the stakeholder, access need, evidence source, constraint, failure, and operating environment. For Publish the incident record · Debug Incident Learning, keep Incident Learning connected to mechanically reviewable stakeholder, affected-user, artifact, changed-case, failure, repair, release, recovery, and transfer decision evidence.

content.title: Evidence defense
content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Preserve timeline and evidence, restore safely, communicate impact, analyze contributing conditions without blame, and track preventive work.
